name: Al-Arqam Islamic School
mission: TO MEET THE EDUCATIONAL NEEDS OF THE MUSLIM CHILDREN LIVING WITHIN THE GREATER SACRAMENTO AREA.
theoryOfChange: Al-Arqam Islamic School has published its 'Student Learning Goals' as part of its Islamic Education curriculum. These goals serve as a results framework, outlining the intended impact on students across five key areas: living Islam, becoming lifelong learners, effective communicators, citizens of the world, and honoring themselves physically and spiritually.
scoreSummary: Al-Arqam Islamic School shows exceptional impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: Western Association of Schools and Colleges (WASC)
Accreditation (Western Association of Schools and Colleges (WASC))

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

zakat.claim_evidence: The donation page for Al-Arqam Islamic School (Sacramento, CA) lists methods for donations (checks, PayPal, Zelle) but does not explicitly mention "zakat" or have a dedicated zakat fund or campaign. (Source: https://www.alarqamislamicschool.org/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=1440031&type=d)

all_citations.claim: The organization reports a program expense ratio of 98.1% and functions as a college preparatory institution.
Total revenue reached $4,330,166 with working capital of 9.1 months and a fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised.
The school provides college preparatory academics and serves the Greater Sacramento area.
The institution offers an International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma Programme.
The school maintains a code of conduct and parent policies focused on Islamic educational values.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a 67/100 due to its outstanding financial efficiency and direct impact on student capacity building, though its alignment score is moderated by its specific regional focus.
areas_for_improvement: Limited alignment for donors specifically seeking high-urgency global humanitarian interventions
Data confidence is medium, suggesting a need for more transparent third-party impact reporting

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the school provides a vital service for the local Muslim community, its alignment score of 24/50 reflects a moderate donor fit for those outside the Sacramento area and a focus on long-term education rather than immediate global crises [3][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The school demonstrates high effectiveness in resource allocation, directing a 98.1% program expense ratio toward its educational mission [1][2]. With total revenue of $4,330,166, the institution maintains a solid financial cushion of 9.1 months of working capital to ensure program continuity [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Al-Arqam Islamic School provides rigorous college preparatory academics and International Baccalaureate programming for Muslim youth in the Greater Sacramento area.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 98.1%, indicating nearly all funds directly support educational services
Strong financial stability with 9.1 months of working capital available
Highly efficient fundraising operations with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: Al-Arqam Islamic School & College Preparatory is dedicated to meeting the educational needs of Muslim children through a curriculum that combines academic excellence with Islamic values [3][6]. The institution operates as a Sadaqah-eligible organization, focusing on capacity building through its International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma Programme and college preparatory tracks [1][4].

case_against.summary: The primary limitation is the <cite id="13">lack of public beneficiary data</cite>, which prevents a granular analysis of cost-effectiveness.

confidence.notes: Strong financial data, but impact reporting is limited to internal academic standards.

alignment.explanation: The school is a strong fit for donors prioritizing <cite id="6">Islamic educational infrastructure</cite> and local community capacity building.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be increased by <cite id="15">expanding services to underserved populations</cite> within the regional Muslim community.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust the institution due to its <cite id="10">100% independent board</cite> and its long-standing <cite id="1">track record since 1998</cite>. The school maintains <cite id="11">low liabilities of only $125,443</cite> against substantial assets.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the school could pursue a Candid Seal of Transparency and publish annual impact reports.

impact.explanation: The school achieves a high impact score through its <cite id="2">IB World School authorization</cite> and a <cite id="5">98.1% program expense ratio</cite>, which is well above the <cite id="8">industry standard of 75%</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="13">tracking long-term student outcomes</cite> and college matriculation rates in public reporting.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value the development of high-quality Islamic educational institutions and want to support rigorous college preparatory tracks for Muslim youth.
donor_motivations: Want to invest in the long-term intellectual capital of the Muslim community
Value established institutions with a 25+ year track record
Prefer supporting schools with high academic standards like the IB program
Seek organizations with zero fundraising overhead
giving_considerations: Donations support local community infrastructure rather than emergency relief
The school is primarily tuition-funded, with donations supplementing operations
Financial efficiency is elite, but impact data is focused on internal student success
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for international humanitarian crisis response.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects the school's <cite id="2">IB World School authorization</cite> and documented <cite id="12">honor codes and academic policies</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The school creates impact by providing a dual-curriculum environment that prepares students for higher education while reinforcing Islamic identity.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Accreditation ensures that the educational services provided meet international standards for college readiness.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The school has a <cite id="16">$5 million funding gap</cite> for its strategic expansion and program enhancements.

strengths.detail: The school directs <cite id="5">100.0% of its expenses toward programs</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="8">peer program ratio median of 89.6%</cite>.
The organization maintains <cite id="7">9.1 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring operational continuity and resilience against economic fluctuations.
As an <cite id="2">authorized IB World School</cite>, the institution offers a globally recognized college preparatory curriculum.

strengths_deep_dive: Al-Arqam Islamic School demonstrates a highly efficient financial model where <cite id="9">fundraising efficiency is $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that the school does not divert tuition or donation funds into aggressive marketing or solicitation. This efficiency is paired with a strong <cite id="10">100% independent board of directors</cite>, which provides objective oversight of the school's <cite id="11">$2,633,655 in total assets</cite>. The school's commitment to academic excellence is codified in its <cite id="12">Student Honor Code and Parent Policy Handbook</cite>, which ensures a disciplined and values-based learning environment.
rich_narrative.summary: Al-Arqam Islamic School & College Preparatory is a specialized educational institution that has served the Greater Sacramento area since its <cite id="1">founding in 1998</cite>. The school provides a comprehensive academic framework that integrates <cite id="2">International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma programming</cite> with Islamic studies and Qur’anic instruction. Operating with a significant <cite id="3">total revenue of $4,155,519</cite>, the organization primarily generates its funding through <cite id="4">program service revenue totaling $3,925,159</cite>, reflecting a sustainable model driven by tuition and educational services. The institution maintains an <cite id="5">exceptional program expense ratio of 98.1%</cite>, ensuring that all functional expenses are directed toward its core mission of student development and academic excellence. Beyond standard academics, the school utilizes a <cite id="6">House System and various student clubs</cite> to foster community and leadership among Muslim youth. Its financial position is notably resilient, characterized by <cite id="7">9.1 months of working capital held in working capital</cite>, which provides a stable foundation for long-term educational planning and institutional growth.
